title: 1.1: Tests that worker.work(options) returns all results combined when all datasources respond successfully
1.2: Tests that worker.work(options) returns only successful results when one source fails
1.3: Tests that worker.work(options) returns results from non-cancelled sources when a source is cancelled during execution
1.4: Tests that worker.work(options) returns successful results when a different source fails
2.1: Places the test for all workers successful in DashboardQueryRunner/AnnotationsWorker.test.ts
2.2: Places the test for legacy worker failure in DashboardQueryRunner/AnnotationsWorker.test.ts
2.3: Places the test for worker cancellation in DashboardQueryRunner/AnnotationsWorker.test.ts
2.4: Places the test for nextgen worker failure in DashboardQueryRunner/AnnotationsWorker.test.ts
3.1: All tests use the Jest framework with RxJS testing patterns
3.2: All tests follow the BDD naming pattern with describe('when [condition]') containing it('then it should [result]')
